explanation: Sodium reacts violently with water. This single replacement reaction produces sodium hydroxide and hydrogen gas, releasing significant heat (exothermic). The hydrogen gas can ignite due to the heat generated.
Hydrogen gas combines with oxygen gas in a highly exothermic synthesis reaction (often explosive) to form water. This combustion produces a flash of light, loud boom, and rapid release of energy.
This is an acid-base neutralization. Hydrochloric acid reacts with sodium hydroxide to form table salt (NaCl) and water. It produces heat but is less violent than sodium and water.
